member.name: Sojan Joseph

addresses: Regus Ashford, The Panorama, Park Street, Ashford, Kent, TN24 8DF

member.synopsis: Sojan Joseph is the Labour MP for Ashford , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking Offences: Fines
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether his department has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing regulations for standard fines in car parks owned by private operators.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is carefully considering options on the level of the private parking charge cap as part of its work to introduce a new private parking Code of Practice. The government intends to lay this new Code before parliament in autumn 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Sewers: Property Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her department has made of the capacity and environmental performance of sewage treatment infrastructure serving new housing developments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government now requires sewerage undertakers to plan ahead to manage drainage and wastewater. This is to ensure they meet future demand, support growth, and protect the environment. These plans will help sewerage undertakers to fully assess the capaci...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Sewage: Pollution Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department has discussed with local planning authorities the potential for allowing alternative on-site wastewater treatment solutions where these may reduce nutrient disch
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government supports innovation in the water sector and works closely with regulators to ensure solutions comply with requirements. Nutrient pollution from wastewater is one of the key pressures affecting our rivers, lakes, and seas. The Government is ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment his department has made of the impact of nutrient neutrality requirements on small-scale residential developments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Impact Assessment for the Planning and Infrastructure Act can be found here.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping: Ashford
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to help tackle fly-tipping in Ashford constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Waste Crime Action Plan sets a zero‑tolerance approach to waste crime and fly-tipping. It includes £45 million for the Environment Agency to double the enforcement, new police-style powers for officers, and budget for tougher sanctions for offenders, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Transport: Land
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent steps she has taken to ensure that (a) thistles and (b) ragwort are removed from land owned by her Department between the village of Mersham and the Sevington inland border facility.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The most appropriate weeding regime is being kept under review. The Department has undertaken the removal of weeds on previous occasions. The field is allocated for a biodiversity scheme, and we need to consider the impact of any weeding regime on the ove...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of extending business rates relief to small businesses that are not in the retail, hospitality or leisure sectors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has introduced new permanently lower multipliers for eligible retail, hospitality and leisure (RHL) properties. These new multipliers are worth nearly £1 billion per year and benefit over 750,000 properties. Since these new multipliers were...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Kent
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to support small and medium sized businesses in a) Ashford constituency and b) Kent.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s Plan for Small Businesses, published in July, sets out the measures that the Government is taking to support small businesses across the UK. This includes tackling late payments, boosting access to finance, and removing red tape. This wil...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Age Assurance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she has made an assessment of the effectiveness of the rules governing data protection that require sites and services using age verification to delete submitted information once tha
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has not undertaken a specific assessment of the effectiveness of rules requiring the deletion of data submitted for age verification once it has been used.The UK’s data protection framework requires personal data to be minimised and not ret...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Pensioners: Income Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent assessment she has had made of the potential merits of extending tax relief for pension contributions for people aged 75 and over.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government wishes to encourage pension saving, to help ensure that people have an income, or funds on which they can draw, throughout retirement. This is why, for the majority of savers, pension contributions are tax-free. This makes pensions tax reli...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Respiratory Diseases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of trends in the level of (a) respiratory disease and (b) the number of emergency hospital admissions for respiratory conditions in (a) Ashford constituency,
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data is available for emergency finished admission episodes (FAEs) where there was a primary diagnosis of 'respiratory conditions’. The following table shows the number of FAEs where there was a primary diagnosis of 'respiratory conditions’ for Ashford an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Allergies: Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that patients with known medical intolerances are able to be dispensed medication that is appropriate for their allergy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Pharmacists are highly trained and skilled healthcare professionals. As part of dispensing medicines, pharmacists have a professional responsibility to inform patients about the medicines they are taking, and this may include mentioning any ingredients in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: People Smuggling: International Cooperation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps her Department is taking with international partners to help tackle organised immigration crime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Immigration crime is an international problem, and it can only be solved through international cooperation.That is why we have worked to develop agreements with France, Germany and Italy and others to break the business model of the criminal gangs, and wh...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to consider the development of national standards for adult social care in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National standards of care will be an integral part of the national care service we are building, so people can rely on consistent, high‑quality care wherever they live.We are already progressing towards this through our three objectives for adult social ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of trends in the level of social care standards across England; and what steps he is taking to support greater consistency of care provision.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National standards of care will be an integral part of the national care service we are building, so people can rely on consistent, high‑quality care wherever they live.We are already progressing towards this through our three objectives for adult social ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Signs and Markings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if her Department has made an assessment of the effectiveness of road signs used by local authorities to help prevent road causalities among (a) hedgehogs and (b) other small mammals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has made no such assessment. Local authorities are responsible for the installation of the small wild animal road signs.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts: First Time Buyers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of increasing the Lifetime ISA property price limit above the current £450,000 threshold for first-time buyers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data from the latest UK House Price Index shows that while the average price paid by first-time buyers has increased, it is still below the Lifetime ISA (Individual Savings Accounts) property price cap in all regions of the UK except for London, where the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what discussions his Department has had with private contractors working in the NHS on trade union recognition for employees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There have been no specific discussions on this matter. The terms and conditions in the standard National Health Service contract, used to contract with private contractors in England, recognises the need to engage with trade unions where they are present...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what discussions his Department has had with private contractors working in the NHS on paying staff the same rates as equivalent NHS staff.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There have been no specific discussions on this with private contractors working in the National Health Service.Independent organisations commissioned by the NHS in England, such as general practices or social enterprises, are free to develop and adapt th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Supply: South East Water
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what discussions her Department has had with South East Water on the continued imposition of a hosepipe ban for its customers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Environment Agency has been in contact with South East Water throughout the drought to ensure the company has followed its drought plan. The decision for removal of the hosepipe ban, is for South East Water as outlined in the sections 76 and 76A-C of ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs
